Fungal infection.emergency_signs: Spreading redness, severe swelling, throbbing pain, pus drainage, or fever.
Fungal infection.home_care: Keep the affected skin clean and dry. Avoid sharing towels or personal items.
Fungal infection.lifestyle: Wear loose, breathable cotton clothes. Shower immediately after sweating.

GERD.emergency_signs: Difficulty swallowing, choking sensations, vomiting blood, or chest pain radiating to arm/jaw.
GERD.home_care: Eat smaller meals. Avoid lying down for at least 2-3 hours after eating.
GERD.lifestyle: Limit acidic, spicy, and fatty foods. Elevate the head of your bed during sleep.

Chronic cholestasis.emergency_signs: Severe abdominal pain, confusion, extreme lethargy, or worsening dark urine and pale stools.
Chronic cholestasis.home_care: Moisturize skin to ease itching. Stay well-hydrated.
Chronic cholestasis.lifestyle: Avoid alcohol. Eat a balanced low-fat diet enriched with fat-soluble vitamins.

Drug Reaction.emergency_signs: Difficulty breathing, swelling of face/lips, blistered skin rash (Stevens-Johnson syndrome), or high fever.
Drug Reaction.home_care: Immediately stop the suspected offending drug and consult your prescribing physician.
Drug Reaction.lifestyle: Keep a written record of drug allergies and wear a medical alert identifier.

Peptic ulcer diseae.emergency_signs: Sudden severe abdominal pain, vomiting blood or coffee-ground material, or black tarry stools.
Peptic ulcer diseae.home_care: Avoid taking NSAIDs (like ibuprofen or aspirin). Eat small bland meals.
Peptic ulcer diseae.lifestyle: Quit smoking and limit alcohol use. Manage psychological stress levels.

AIDS.emergency_signs: Severe shortness of breath, unexplained seizures, sudden high fever, or confusion.
AIDS.home_care: Strictly adhere to antiretroviral therapy (ART) schedule. Prevent infections by washing hands.
AIDS.lifestyle: Consume a nutrient-dense diet and maintain safe sex practices.

Diabetes.emergency_signs: Fruity breath odor, rapid breathing, confusion, vomiting, or extremely high/low glucose levels.
Diabetes.home_care: Monitor blood glucose regularly. Carry fast-acting glucose tablets for emergencies.
Diabetes.lifestyle: Adopt a low-glycemic, fiber-rich diet. Engage in regular physical activity.

Gastroenteritis.emergency_signs: Inability to keep liquids down for 24 hours, extreme dizziness, blood in stool, or high fever.
Gastroenteritis.home_care: Sip Oral Rehydration Salts (ORS) frequently. Eat plain foods like bananas, rice, applesauce.
Gastroenteritis.lifestyle: Wash hands thoroughly with soap. Disinfect contaminated household surfaces.

Bronchial Asthma.emergency_signs: Severe wheezing, blue lips/nails, chest tightness where rescue inhaler provides no relief.
Bronchial Asthma.home_care: Keep your rescue inhaler accessible at all times. Sit upright during an attack.
Bronchial Asthma.lifestyle: Identify and avoid triggers like pollen, dust mites, pet dander, and cold air.

Hypertension.emergency_signs: Severe headache, chest pain, shortness of breath, blurry vision, or sudden weakness/numbness.
Hypertension.home_care: Check blood pressure daily. Take prescribed medications exactly as directed.
Hypertension.lifestyle: Limit sodium intake to under 1,500mg daily. Exercise regularly and limit alcohol.

tests: Brain MRI (to rule out other causes)
Migraine.emergency_signs: Sudden 'thunderclap' headache, fever, neck stiffness, confusion, or difficulty speaking.
Migraine.home_care: Rest in a dark, quiet room. Apply a cool compress to the forehead.
Migraine.lifestyle: Maintain a headache diary. Eat meals and sleep at consistent times every day.

Cervical spondylosis.emergency_signs: Loss of bladder or bowel control, severe numbness/weakness in arms or legs, or balance issues.
Cervical spondylosis.home_care: Perform neck stretches regularly. Maintain an ergonomic workspace configuration.
Cervical spondylosis.lifestyle: Avoid looking down at mobile phones for extended periods. Sleep with a supportive pillow.

Paralysis (brain hemorrhage).emergency_signs: FAST signs: Face dropping, Arm weakness, Speech difficulty -> Time to call emergency.
Paralysis (brain hemorrhage).home_care: Requires immediate emergency hospital admission. Do not give any food or liquids.
Paralysis (brain hemorrhage).lifestyle: Manage high blood pressure carefully. Stop smoking and limit alcohol intake.

Jaundice.emergency_signs: High fever with chills, severe right-sided abdominal pain, confusion, or dark blood in stool.
Jaundice.home_care: Stay hydrated and avoid liver-taxing medications like paracetamol/acetaminophen.
Jaundice.lifestyle: Eat a light low-fat diet. Completely avoid alcohol consumption.

Malaria.emergency_signs: Confusion, seizures, breathing difficulties, extreme fatigue, or yellowing of the eyes.
Malaria.home_care: Take prescribed antimalarial medications exactly as instructed by a physician.
Malaria.lifestyle: Use mosquito nets, insect repellents, and wear long sleeves during evening hours.

Chicken pox.emergency_signs: Rash spreading to eyes, severe cough, extreme drowsiness, or secondary bacterial infection.
Chicken pox.home_care: Use calamine lotion to soothe itching. Rest and avoid scratching blisters.
Chicken pox.lifestyle: Quarantine to avoid spreading the virus. Get vaccinated if eligible.

Dengue.emergency_signs: Severe abdominal pain, persistent vomiting, bleeding gums, blood in vomit, or rapid drop in platelets.
Dengue.home_care: Stay hydrated. Take paracetamol for fever; DO NOT take ibuprofen or aspirin.
Dengue.lifestyle: Eliminate standing water around the home to prevent mosquito breeding.

Typhoid.emergency_signs: Severe abdominal pain with swelling, blood in stool, persistent high fever, or confusion.
Typhoid.home_care: Complete the entire course of prescribed antibiotics. Hydrate using clean, boiled water.
Typhoid.lifestyle: Avoid raw foods, street foods, and drinks containing unpurified ice.

hepatitis A.emergency_signs: Persistent vomiting, extreme sleepiness, yellowing of skin/eyes, or bleeding issues.
hepatitis A.home_care: Rest and avoid alcohol. Eat small, frequent meals if nauseous.
hepatitis A.lifestyle: Practice strict hand hygiene. Get the Hepatitis A vaccine.

Hepatitis B.emergency_signs: Severe abdominal pain, swelling of legs/abdomen, confusion, or dark vomit.
Hepatitis B.home_care: Avoid self-prescribed medications. Keep regular appointments for monitoring.
Hepatitis B.lifestyle: Do not share personal grooming items like razors or toothbrushes.

Hepatitis C.emergency_signs: Confusion, sudden high fever, vomiting blood, or swelling in the legs/ankles.
Hepatitis C.home_care: Follow your direct-acting antiviral medication plan. Minimize liver strain.
Hepatitis C.lifestyle: Never share needles or syringes. Get checked for comorbidities.

Hepatitis D.emergency_signs: Rapidly worsening jaundice, confusion, severe lethargy, or bruising/bleeding.
Hepatitis D.home_care: Manage Hepatitis B infection since HDV requires HBV to replicate.
Hepatitis D.lifestyle: Avoid any high-risk exposures. Consult a transplant center if severe.

Hepatitis E.emergency_signs: Persistent vomiting, extreme drowsiness, yellow eyes, especially critical if pregnant.
Hepatitis E.home_care: Stay hydrated and rest. Avoid pregnancy complications via close monitoring.
Hepatitis E.lifestyle: Drink only safe, boiled or bottled water. Avoid undercooked pork/wild game.

Alcoholic hepatitis.emergency_signs: Severe abdominal swelling, confusion, vomiting blood, or sudden high fever.
Alcoholic hepatitis.home_care: Complete and absolute cessation of alcohol consumption is mandatory.
Alcoholic hepatitis.lifestyle: Eat a high-protein, calorie-rich diet under medical guidance.

Tuberculosis.emergency_signs: Coughing up large amounts of blood, severe chest pain, or sudden shortness of breath.
Tuberculosis.home_care: Strict adherence to the 6-month anti-TB drug schedule is essential to prevent resistance.
Tuberculosis.lifestyle: Wear a mask to protect others. Ensure good ventilation in living areas.

tests: Rapid influenza test (to rule out flu)
Common Cold.emergency_signs: Difficulty breathing, wheezing, high persistent fever, or severe ear pain.
Common Cold.home_care: Stay hydrated, rest, and use saline nasal drops. Take warm liquids.
Common Cold.lifestyle: Wash hands frequently. Cover your mouth when coughing or sneezing.

Pneumonia.emergency_signs: Difficulty breathing, chest pain when breathing, confusion (especially in elderly), or blue lips.
Pneumonia.home_care: Complete all prescribed antibiotics. Rest and use a humidifier.
Pneumonia.lifestyle: Avoid tobacco smoke. Get the pneumococcal vaccine.

Dimorphic hemmorhoids(piles).emergency_signs: Severe persistent rectal bleeding, extreme pain, or dizziness/faintness from blood loss.
Dimorphic hemmorhoids(piles).home_care: Take warm sitz baths. Avoid straining during bowel movements.
Dimorphic hemmorhoids(piles).lifestyle: Eat a high-fiber diet, drink plenty of water, and stay active.

Heart attack.emergency_signs: Crushing chest pain radiating to arm, neck, or jaw; shortness of breath; sweating; cold clammy skin.
Heart attack.home_care: This is a life-threatening emergency. Call ambulance immediately. Chew an aspirin if advised by dispatch.
Heart attack.lifestyle: Follow a heart-healthy diet. Avoid smoking and exercise regularly after recovery.

Varicose veins.emergency_signs: Sudden swelling of one leg, redness, warmth, or chest pain/shortness of breath (signs of DVT/PE).
Varicose veins.home_care: Wear compression stockings. Elevate legs above heart level when resting.
Varicose veins.lifestyle: Avoid standing or sitting for long periods. Exercise to improve calf muscle tone.

Hypothyroidism.emergency_signs: Extreme fatigue, confusion, low body temperature, or swelling of the face/tongue.
Hypothyroidism.home_care: Take levothyroxine daily on an empty stomach as prescribed by your doctor.
Hypothyroidism.lifestyle: Eat a fiber-rich diet to combat constipation. Exercise regularly.

tests: TSH, Free T3, and Free T4 tests
Thyroid scan and radioactive iodine uptake
Hyperthyroidism.emergency_signs: Rapid heart rate (arrhythmia), fever, confusion, vomiting, or extreme agitation (Thyroid Storm).
Hyperthyroidism.home_care: Monitor heart rate. Take beta-blockers or antithyroid medicines as prescribed.
Hyperthyroidism.lifestyle: Manage stress, eat calorie-dense foods if losing weight rapidly, and avoid excess iodine.

Hypoglycemia.emergency_signs: Confusion, loss of consciousness, seizures, or inability to swallow safely.
Hypoglycemia.home_care: Apply the 15-15 rule: consume 15g fast sugar, wait 15 minutes, check blood glucose.
Hypoglycemia.lifestyle: Eat regular, balanced meals. Always carry fast-acting carbohydrates with you.

tests: Joint fluid analysis (to rule out infection)
Osteoarthristis.emergency_signs: Inability to bear weight on the joint, sudden severe swelling, warmth, or redness.
Osteoarthristis.home_care: Apply ice packs for swelling and warm packs for stiffness. Use joint supports.
Osteoarthristis.lifestyle: Maintain a healthy weight to reduce joint load. Do low-impact exercises like swimming.

Arthritis.emergency_signs: High fever accompanied by joint swelling (Septic arthritis), or severe joint pain with chills.
Arthritis.home_care: Balance rest with gentle range-of-motion exercises. Use assistive devices.
Arthritis.lifestyle: Adopt an anti-inflammatory diet (rich in omega-3). Avoid joint strain.

(vertigo) Paroymsal Positional Vertigo.emergency_signs: Vertigo with double vision, slurred speech, limb weakness, difficulty walking, or chest pain.
(vertigo) Paroymsal Positional Vertigo.home_care: Sit down immediately during a vertigo episode. Perform the Epley maneuver under guidance.
(vertigo) Paroymsal Positional Vertigo.lifestyle: Avoid sudden neck movements and sleeping flat on your back.

Acne.emergency_signs: Extremely painful, deep nodules or cysts with signs of spreading infection/fever.
Acne.home_care: Wash skin gently twice daily. Avoid squeezing or popping pimples.
Acne.lifestyle: Use non-comedogenic cosmetics. Wash hair regularly to reduce oils.

Urinary tract infection.emergency_signs: High fever, flank/lower back pain, chills, nausea, vomiting (signs of kidney infection).
Urinary tract infection.home_care: Drink plenty of water to flush out bacteria. Avoid carbonated/caffeinated drinks.
Urinary tract infection.lifestyle: Practice good hygiene. Wipe front to back and urinate after intercourse.

Psoriasis.emergency_signs: Pustules all over the body, red skin peeling, chills, fever (Erythrodermic psoriasis emergency).
Psoriasis.home_care: Apply prescribed emollients regularly. Take warm baths using colloidal oatmeal.
Psoriasis.lifestyle: Identify and avoid triggers like stress, skin injuries, and smoking.

Impetigo.emergency_signs: Rash spreading rapidly, red streaks extending from the sores, or high fever.
Impetigo.home_care: Keep sores covered with clean gauze. Wash hands thoroughly with soap.
Impetigo.lifestyle: Avoid sharing clothes or linens with infected individuals to prevent transmission.

Allergy.emergency_signs: Difficulty breathing, swelling of face/lips/tongue, hives all over, dizziness, or loss of consciousness (Anaphylaxis).
Allergy.home_care: Avoid known allergens. Take over-the-counter antihistamines as advised by a doctor.
Allergy.lifestyle: Keep windows closed during high pollen seasons. Wash bedding at high temperature. Use air purifiers.
